title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your manager has asked you to write a report on the experience of staff who have been working from home over the past year. Include information about the benefits they have found, any difficulties they have experienced, and recommendations for the future.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Remote Working Experience — Staff Survey Results

Introduction
This report summarises feedback gathered from 80 members of staff who have been working from home over the past twelve months, with the aim of informing future workplace policy.

Benefits Identified
The majority of respondents (78%) reported a significant improvement in work-life balance, citing the elimination of daily commutes as particularly beneficial. Productivity was also reported to have increased, with 65% of staff stating they complete more tasks at home than in the office. Many employees also appreciated the flexibility to manage their own time.

Difficulties Experienced
Despite the positives, 55% of staff expressed concerns about feeling isolated from colleagues. Several employees mentioned difficulties separating work from personal life at home, and some reported inadequate equipment or internet connections.

Recommendations
Based on these findings, a hybrid model is recommended, allowing staff to work from home two to three days per week while maintaining regular in-office days to support collaboration and team wellbeing. The company should also provide equipment subsidies for home offices.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'Have you used a productivity app or tool that has helped you work or study from home more effectively? Write a review describing the tool and saying whether you would recommend it to people working remotely.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
App Review: Notion — The All-in-One Workspace

Notion is a highly flexible productivity and note-taking application that has become extremely popular among remote workers and students alike. It combines to-do lists, databases, calendars, and documents into a single, customisable workspace.

What sets Notion apart from competitors is its adaptability. You can build an entire project management system, a personal diary, or a study planner — all within the same app. Templates are available for almost any use case, which makes getting started surprisingly quick.

For remote workers, the ability to share pages and collaborate in real time is invaluable. My team uses Notion to track ongoing projects, share meeting notes, and manage deadlines — and it has genuinely transformed how we work together online.

The learning curve can be steep initially, and the free version has some limitations for larger teams. However, the paid plans are reasonably priced given the functionality offered.

For anyone struggling with organisation while working from home, Notion is an excellent investment. Rating: 4.5/5

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Working from home has become the new normal for many people. Write an article discussing the advantages and disadvantages of remote work and sharing your personal opinion on whether it is a positive development.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Home Sweet Office: The Remote Working Revolution

Just a few years ago, working from home was considered a rare privilege. Today, for millions of people around the world, it has become everyday reality. But is this shift truly for the better?

The advantages are undeniable. Remote workers save time and money by avoiding daily commutes, enjoy greater flexibility over their schedules, and often report feeling less stressed. For parents and carers especially, the ability to work from home can be life-changing.

However, the downsides should not be ignored. Working in isolation can lead to loneliness, and the absence of a clear boundary between work and home life can cause burnout. Many people also miss the spontaneous conversations and collaborative energy of an office environment.

Personally, I believe the ideal solution is a hybrid model — working from home several days a week while still maintaining regular in-person contact with colleagues. This approach offers the best of both worlds and reflects how many companies are now organising their workforce.

The future of work is flexible. And that, I think, is a very good thing.
